author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter IV. 1–42.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is nothing new to your ears, beloved, that the Evangelist John, like an eagle, takes a loftier flight, and soars above the dark mist of earth, to gaze with steadier eyes upon the light of truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From his Gospel much has already been treated of and discussed through our ministry, with the Lord’s help; and the passage which has been read to-day follows in due order.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What I am about to say, with the Lord’s permission, many of you will hear in such wise that you will be reviewing what you know, rather than learning what you know not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet, for all that, your attention ought not to be slack, because it is not an acquiring, but a reviewing, of knowledge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This has been read, and we have in our hands to discourse upon this passage—that which the Lord Jesus spoke with the Samaritan woman at Jacob’s well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The things spoken there are great mysteries, and the similitudes of great things; feeding the hungry, and refreshing the weary soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now when the Lord knew this, “when He had heard that the Pharisees had learned that He was making more disciples than John, and baptized more (though Jesus baptized not, but His disciples), He left Judea, and departed again into Galilee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We must not discourse of this too long, lest, by dwelling on what is manifest, we shall lack the time to investigate and lay open what is obscure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly, if the Lord saw that the fact of their coming to know that He made more disciples, and baptized more, would so avail to salvation to the Pharisees in following Him, as to become themselves His disciples, and to desire to be baptized by Him; rather would He not have left Judea, but would have remained there for their sakes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because He knew their knowledge of the fact, and at the same time knew their envy, and that they learned this, not to follow, but to persecute him, He departed thence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He could, indeed, even when present, cause that He should not be taken of them, if He would not; He had it in His power not to be put to death, if He would not, since He had the power not to be born, if He would not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because, in everything that He did as man, He was showing an example to them who were to believe on Him (that any one servant of God sinneth not if he retire into another place, when he sees, it may be, the rage of his persecutors, or of them that seek to bring his soul into evil; but if a servant of God did this he might appear to commit sin, had not the Lord led the way in doing it), that good Master did this to teach us, not because He feared it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It may perhaps surprise you why it is said, that “Jesus baptized more than John;” and after this was said, it is subjoined, “although Jesus baptized not, but His disciples.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was the statement made false, and then corrected by this addition?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, are both true, viz. that Jesus both did and also did not baptize?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did in fact baptize, because it was He that cleansed; and He did not baptize, because it was not He that touched.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The disciples supplied the ministry of the body; He afforded the aid of His majesty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, when could He cease from baptizing, so long as He ceased not from cleansing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of Him it is said by the same John, in the person of the Baptist, who saith, “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus, therefore, is still baptizing; and so long as we continue to be baptized, Jesus baptizeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let a man come without fear to the minister below; for he has a Master above.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it may be one saith, Christ does indeed baptize, but in spirit, not in body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if, indeed, it were by the gift of another than He that any is imbued even with the sacrament of corporal and visible baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou know that it is He that baptizeth, not only with the Spirit, but also with water?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the apostle: “Even as Christ,” saith he, “loved the Church, and gave Himself for it, purifying it with the washing of water by the Word, that He might present to Himself a glorious Church, not having spot, or wrinkle, or any such thing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Purifying it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “With the washing of water by the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the baptism of Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The washing of water by the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Take away the water, it is no baptism; take away the Word, it is no baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This much, then, on the preliminary circumstances, by occasion of which He came to a conversation with that woman, let us look at the matters that remain; matters full of mysteries and pregnant with sacraments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And He must needs pass through Samaria.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He cometh then to a city of Samaria which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground which Jacob gave to his son Joseph.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Jacob’s fountain was there.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was a well; but every well is a fountain, yet not every fountain a well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where the water flows from the earth, and offers itself for use to them that draw it, it is called a fountain; but if accessible, and on the surface, it is called only a fountain: if, however, it be deep and far down, it is called a well, but in such wise as not to lose the name of fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus therefore, being wearied with His journey, sat thus on the well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was about the sixth hour.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now begin the mysteries.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not without a purpose that Jesus is weary; not indeed without a purpose that the strength of God is weary; not without a purpose that He is weary, by whom the wearied are refreshed; not without a purpose is He weary, by whose absence we are wearied, by whose presence we are strengthened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless Jesus is weary, and weary with His journey; and He sits down, and that, too, near a well; and it is at the sixth hour that, being wearied, He sits down.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these things hint something, are intended to intimate something, they make us eager, and encourage us to knock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May Himself open to us and to you; He who has deigned to exhort us, so as to say, “Knock, and it shall be opened to you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was for thee that Jesus was wearied with His journey.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We find Jesus to be strength, and we find Jesus to be weak: we find a strong and a weak Jesus: strong, because “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God: the same was in the beginning with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou see how this Son of God is strong?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made:” and without labor, too, were they made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then what can be stronger than He, by whom all things were made without labor?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou know Him weak?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The strength of Christ created thee, the weakness of Christ created thee anew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The strength of Christ caused that to be which was not: the weakness of Christ caused that what was should not perish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He fashioned us by His strength, He sought us by His weakness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As weak, then, He nourishes the weak, as a hen her chickens; for He likened Him self to a hen: “How often,” He saith to Jerusalem, “would I have gathered thy children under my wings, as a hen her chickens; but thou wouldest not!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And you see, brethren, how a hen becomes weak with her chickens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No other bird, when it is a mother, is recognized at once to be so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We see all kinds of sparrows building their nests before our eyes; we see swallows, storks, doves, every day building their nests; but we do not know them to be parents, except when we see them on their nests.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the hen is so enfeebled over her brood, that even if the chickens are not following her, if thou see not the young ones, yet thou knowest her at once to be a mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With her wings drooping, her feathers ruffled, her note hoarse, in all her limbs she becomes so sunken and abject, that, as I have said, even though thou seest not her young, yet thou perceivest her to be a mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In such manner was Jesus weak, wearied with His journey.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His journey is the flesh assumed for us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how can He, who is present everywhere, have a journey, He who is nowhere absent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither does He go, or whence, but that He could not come to us, except He had assumed the form of visible flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, as He deigned to come to us in such manner, that He appeared in the form of a servant by the flesh assumed, that same assumption of flesh is His journey.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, “wearied with His journey,” what else is it but wearied in the flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus was weak in the flesh: but do not thou become weak; but in His weakness be strong, because what is “the weakness of God is stronger than men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Under this image of things, Adam, who was the figure of Him that was to be, afforded us a great indication of this mystery; rather, God afforded it in him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he was deemed worthy to receive a wife while he slept, and that wife was made for him of his own rib: since from Christ, sleeping on the cross, was the Church to come,—from His side, namely, as He slept; for it was from His side, pierced with the spear, as He hung on the cross, that the sacraments of the Church flowed forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why have I chosen to say this, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it is the weakness of Christ that makes us strong.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A remarkable figure of this went before in the case of Adam.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God could have taken flesh from the man to make of it a woman, and it seems that this might have been the more suitable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was the weaker sex that was being made, and weakness ought to have been made of flesh rather than of bone; for the bones are the stronger parts in the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He took not flesh to make of it a woman; but took a bone, and of the bone was the woman shaped, and flesh was filled in into the place of the bone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He could have restored bone for bone; He could have taken, not a rib, but flesh, for the making of the woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, did this signify?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Woman was made, as it were, strong, from the rib; Adam was made, as it were, weak, from the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is Christ and the Church; His weakness is our strength.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why at the sixth hour?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because at the sixth age of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the Gospel, count up as an hour each, the first age from Adam to Noah; the second, from Noah to Abraham; the third, from Abraham to David; the fourth, from David to the removing to Babylon; the fifth, from the removing to Babylon to the baptism of John: thence is the sixth being enacted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why dost thou marvel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus came, and, by humbling Himself, came to a well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came wearied, because He carried weak flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At the sixth hour, because in the sixth age of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To a well, because to the depth of this our habitation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For which reason it is said in the psalm: “From the depth have I cried unto Thee, O Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He sat, as I said, because He was humbled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And there came a woman.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Figure of the Church not yet justified, but now about to be justified: for this is the subject of the discourse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She comes ignorant, she finds Him, and there is a dealing with her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us see what, and wherefore.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Samaritans did not belong to the nation of the Jews: they were foreigners, though they inhabited neighboring lands.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It would take a long time to relate the origin of the Samaritans; that we may not be detained by long discourse of this, and leave necessary matters unsaid, suffice to say, then, that we regard the Samaritans as aliens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, lest you should think that I have said this with more boldness than truth, hear the Lord Jesus Himself, what He said of that Samaritan, one of the ten lepers whom He had cleansed, who alone returned to give thanks: “Were there not ten cleansed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where are the nine?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There was not another to give glory to God, save this stranger.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is pertinent to the image of the reality, that this woman, who bore the type of the Church, comes of strangers: for the Church was to come of the Gentiles, an alien from the race of the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that woman, then, let us hear ourselves, and in her acknowledge ourselves, and in her give thanks to God for ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For she was the figure, not the reality; for she both first showed forth the figure and became the reality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For she believed on Him who, of her, set the figure before us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “She cometh, then, to draw water.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had simply come to draw water, as people are wont to do, be they men or women.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For His disciples were gone away into the city to buy meat.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then saith the Samaritan woman unto Him, How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, who am a Samaritan woman?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see that they were aliens: indeed, the Jews would not use their vessels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as the woman brought with her a vessel with which to draw the water, it made her wonder that a Jew sought drink of her,—a thing which the Jews were not accustomed to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He who was asking drink was thirsting for the faith of the woman herself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At length, hear who it is that asketh drink: “Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink, thou wouldest, it may be, have asked of Him, and He would have given thee living water.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He asks to drink, and promises to give drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He longs as one about to receive; He abounds as one about to satisfy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If thou knewest,” saith He, “the gift of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The gift of God is the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as yet He speaks to the woman guardedly, and enters into her heart by degrees.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It may be He is now teaching her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what can be sweeter and kinder than that exhortation?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If thou knewest the gift of God,” etc.: thus far He keeps her in suspense.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is commonly called living water which issues from a spring: that which is collected from rain in pools and cisterns is not called living water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it may have flowed from a spring; yet if it should stand collected in some place, not admitting to it that from which it flowed, but, with the course interrupted, separated, as it were, from the channel of the fountain, it is not called “living water:” but that is called living water which is taken as it flows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such water there was in that fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, did He promise to give that which He was asking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The woman, however, being in suspense, saith to Him, “Lord, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how she understood the living water, simply the water which was in that fountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thou wouldst give me living water, and I carry that with which to draw, and thou dost not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The living water is here; how art thou to give it me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understanding another thing, and taking it carnally, she does in a manner knock, that the Master may open up that which is closed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She was knocking in ignorance, not with earnest purpose; she is still an object of pity, not yet of instruction.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord speaks somewhat more clearly of that living water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now the woman had said, “Art thou greater than our father Jacob, who gave us the well, and drank of it himself, his children, and his cattle?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou canst not give me of the living water of this well, because thou hast nothing to draw with: perhaps thou promisest another fountain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Canst thou be better than our father, who dug this well, and used it himself, and his?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Lord, then, declare what He called living water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered and said unto her, Every one that drinketh of this water shall thirst again: but he that drinketh of the water that I shall give him, shall not thirst forever; but the water which I shall give him will become in him a fountain of water, springing up into everlasting life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord has spoken more openly: “It shall become in him a fountain of water, springing up into everlasting life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that drinketh of this water shall not thirst forever.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more evident than that it was not visible, but invisible water, that He was promising?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What more evident than that He was speaking, not in a carnal, but in a spiritual sense?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Still, however, the woman has her mind on the flesh: she is delighted with the thought of thirsting no more, and fancies that this was promised to her by the Lord after a carnal sense; which it will be indeed, but in the resurrection of the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She desired this now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God had indeed granted once to His servant Elias, that during forty days he neither hungered nor thirsted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Could not He give this always, seeing He had power to give it during forty days?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She, however, sighed for it, desiring to have no want, no toil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To be always coming to that fountain, to be burdened with a weight with which to supply her want, and, when that which she had drawn is spent, to be obliged to return again: this was a daily toil to her; because that want of hers was to be relieved, not extinguished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such a gift as Jesus promised delighted her; she asks Him to give her living water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, let us not overlook the fact that it is something spiritual that the Lord was promising.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means, “Whoso shall drink of this water shall thirst again?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is true as to this water; it is true as to what the water signified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since the water in the well is the pleasure of the world in its dark depth: from this men draw it with the vessel of lusts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Stooping forward, they let down the lust to reach the pleasure fetched from the depth of the well, and enjoy the pleasure and the preceding lust let down to fetch it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he who has not despatched his lust in advance cannot get to the pleasure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider lust, then, as the vessel; and pleasure as the water from the depth of the well: when one has got at the pleasure of this world, it is meat to him, it is drink, it is a bath, a show, an amour; can it be that he will not thirst again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “Whoso shall drink of this water,” saith He, “will thirst again;” but if he shall receive water of me, “he shall never thirst.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “We shall be satisfied,” it saith, “with the good things of Thy house.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what water, then, is He to give, but of that of which it is said, “With Thee is the fountain of life”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how shall they thirst, who “shall be drunk with the fatness of Thy house”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What He was promising them was a certain feeding and abundant fullness of the Holy Spirit: but the woman did not yet understand; and not understanding, how did she answer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The woman saith unto Him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Want forced her to labor, and her weakness was pleading against the toil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would that she heard the invitation, “Come unto me, all ye that labor and are heavy laden, and I will refresh you!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is, in fact, what Jesus was saying to her, that she might no longer labor: but she did not yet understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At length, wishing her to understand, “Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means this, “Call thy husband”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it through her husband that He wished to give her that water?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, because she did not understand, did He wish to teach her through her husband?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps it was as the apostle says concerning women, “If they wish to learn anything, let them ask their husbands at home.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this the apostle says of that where there is no Jesus present to teach.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is said, in short, to women whom the apostle was forbidding to speak in the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the Lord Himself was at hand, and in person speaking to her, what need was there that He should speak to her by her husband?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it through her husband that he spoke to Mary, while sitting at His feet and receiving His word; while Martha, wholly occupied with much serving, murmured at the happiness of her sister?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, my brethren, let us hear and understand what it is that the Lord says to the woman, “Call thy husband.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it may be that He is saying also to our soul, “Call thy husband.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us inquire also concerning the soul’s husband.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, is not Jesus Himself already the soul’s real husband?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the understanding be present, since what we are about to say can hardly be apprehended but by attentive hearers: therefore let the understanding be present to apprehend, and perhaps that same understanding will be found to be the husband of the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Jesus, seeing that the woman did not understand, and willing her to understand, says to her, “Call thy husband.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the reason why thou knowest not what I say is, because thy understanding is not present: I am speaking after the Spirit, and thou art hearing after the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The things which I speak relate neither to the pleasure of the ears, nor to the eyes, nor to the smell, nor to the taste, nor to the touch; by the mind alone are they received, by the understanding alone are they drawn up: that understanding is not with thee, how canst thou apprehend what I am saying?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: ‘Call thy husband,’ bring thy understanding forward.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it for thee to have a soul?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not much, for a beast has a soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherein art thou better than the beast?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In having understanding, which the beast has not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then what is “Call thy husband”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thou dost not apprehend me, thou dost not understand me: I am speaking to thee of the gift of God, and thy thought is of the flesh; thou wishest not to thirst in a carnal sense, I am addressing myself to the spirit: thy understanding is absent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: ‘Call thy husband.’

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be not as the horse and mule, which have no understanding.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, to have a soul, and not to have understanding, that is, not to use it, not to live according to it, is a beast’s life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we have somewhat in common with the beasts, that by which we live in the flesh, but it must be ruled by the understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the motions of the soul, which moves after the flesh, and longs to run unrestrainedly loose after carnal delights, are ruled over by the understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Which is to be called the husband?—that which rules, or that which is ruled?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Without doubt, when the life is well ordered the understanding rules the soul, for itself belongs to the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the understanding is not something other than the soul, but a thing of the soul: as the eye is not something other than the flesh, but a thing of the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But whilst the eye is a thing of the flesh, yet it alone enjoys the light; and the other fleshy members may be steeped in light, but they cannot feel the light: the eye alone is both bathed in it, and enjoys it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus in our soul there is a something called the understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This something of the soul, which is called understanding and mind, is enlightened by the higher light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now that higher light, by which the human mind is enlightened, is God; for “that was the true light which enlighteneth every man coming into this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such a light was Christ, such a light was speaking with the woman: yet she was not present with the understanding, to have it enlightened with that light; not merely to have it shed upon it, but to enjoy it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the Lord said, “Call thy husband,” as if He were to say, I wish to enlighten, and yet there is not here whom I may enlighten: bring hither the understanding through which thou mayest be taught, by which thou mayest be ruled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, put the soul without the understanding for the woman; and having the understanding as having the husband.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this husband does not rule the wife well, except when he is ruled by a higher.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the head of the woman is the man, but the head of the man is Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The head of the man was talking with the woman, and the man was not present.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so the Lord, as if He said, Bring hither thy head, that he may receive his head, says, “Call thy husband, and come hither;” that is, Be here, be present: for thou art as absent, while thou understandest not the voice of the Truth here present; be thou present here, but not alone; be thou here with thy husband.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, the husband being not yet called, still she does not understand, still she minds the flesh; for the man is absent: “I have not,” saith she, “a husband.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord proceeds and utters mysteries.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou mayest understand that woman really to have had at that time no husband; she was living with some man, not a lawful husband, rather a paramour than a husband.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord said to her, “Thou hast well said, I have not a husband.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then didst Thou say, “Call thy husband”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now hear how the Lord knew well that she had not a husband.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He says to her,” etc. In case the woman might suppose that the Lord had said, “Thou hast well said, I have not a husband,” just because He had learned this fact of her, and not because he knew it by His own divinity, hear something which thou hast not said: “For thou hast had five husbands, and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband; this thou hast said truly.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Once more He urges us to investigate the matter somewhat more exactly concerning these five husbands.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many have in fact understood, not indeed absurdly, nor so far improbably, the five husbands of this woman to mean the five books of Moses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Samaritans’ made use of these books, and were under the same law: for it was from it they had circumcision.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since we are hemmed in by what follows, “And he whom thou now hast is not thy husband,” it appears to me that we can more easily take the five senses of the body to be the five former husbands of the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when one is born, before he can make use of the mind and reason, he is ruled only by the senses of the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a little child, the soul seeks for or shuns what is heard, and seen, and smells, and tastes, and is perceived by the touch.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It seeks for whatever soothes, and shuns whatever offends, those five senses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At first, the soul lives according to these five senses, as five husbands; because it is ruled by them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why are they called husbands?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because they are lawful and right: made indeed by God, and are the gifts of God to the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul is still weak while ruled by these five husbands, and living under these five husbands; but when she comes to years of exercising reason, if she is taken in hand by the noble discipline and teaching of wisdom, these five men are succeeded in their rule by no other than the true and lawful husband, and one better than they, who both rules better and rules for eternity, who cultivates and instructs her for eternity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the five senses rule us, not for eternity, but for those temporal things that are to be sought or shunned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the understanding, imbued by wisdom, begins to rule the soul, it knows now not only how to avoid a pit, and to walk on even ground—a thing which the eyes show to the soul even in its weakness; nor merely to be charmed with musical voices, and to repel harsh sounds; nor to delight in agreeable scents, and to refuse offensive smells; nor to be captivated by sweetness, and displeased with bitterness; nor to be soothed with what is soft, and hurt with what is rough.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all these things are necessary to the soul in its weakness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then what rule is made use of by that understanding?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not one to discern between black and white, but between just and unjust, between good and evil, between the profitable and the unprofitable, between chastity and impurity, that it may love the one and avoid the other; between charity and hatred, to be in the one, not to be in the other.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This husband had not yet succeeded to those five husbands in that woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where he does not succeed, error sways.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the soul has begun to be capable of reason, it is ruled either by the wise mind or by error: but yet error does not rule but destroys.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, after these five senses was that woman still wandering, and error was tossing her to and fro.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this error was not a lawful husband, but a paramour: for that reason the Lord saith to her, “Thou hast well said, I have not a husband.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou hast had five husbands.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The five senses of the flesh ruled thee at first; thou art come to the age of using reason, and yet thou art not come to wisdom, but art fallen into error.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, after those five husbands, “this whom thou now hast is not thy husband.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if not a husband, what was he but a paramour?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, “Call,” not the paramour, but “thy husband,” that thou mayest receive me with the understanding, and not by error have some false notion of me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the woman was still in error, as she was thinking of that water; whilst the Lord was now speaking of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why was she erring, but because she had a paramour, not a husband?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Put away, therefore, that paramour who corrupts thee, and “go, call thy husband.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Call, and come that thou mayest understand me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The woman saith unto Him, Sir, I see that thou art a prophet.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The husband begins to come, he is not yet fully come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She accounted the Lord a prophet, and a prophet indeed He was; for it was of Himself He said, that “a prophet is not without honor, save in his own country.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, of Him it was said to Moses, “A Prophet will I raise up to them of their brethren, like unto thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Like, namely, as to the form of the flesh, but not in the eminence of His majesty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly we find the Lord Jesus called a Prophet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence this woman is now not far wrong.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I see,” she saith, “that thou art a prophet.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She begins to call the husband, and to shut out the paramour; she begins to ask about a matter that is wont to disquiet her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there was a contention between the Samaritans and the Jews, because the Jews worshipped God in the temple built by Solomon; but the Samaritans, being situated at a distance from it, did not worship there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this reason the Jews, because they worshipped God in the temple, boasted themselves to be better than the Samaritans.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans:” because the latter said to them, How is it you boast and account yourselves to be better than we, just because you have a temple which we have not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did our fathers, who were pleasing to God, worship in that temple?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it not in this mountain where we are they worshipped?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We then do better, say they, who pray to God in this mountain, where our fathers prayed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Both peoples contended in ignorance, because they had not the husband: they were inflated against each other, on the one side in behalf of the temple, on the other in behalf of the mountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, however, does the Lord teach the woman now, as one whose husband has begun to be present?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The woman saith unto Him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Church will come, as it is said in the Song of Songs, “will come, and will pass over from the beginning of faith.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She will come in order to pass through; and pass through she cannot, except from the beginning of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly she now hears, the husband being present: “Woman, believe me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there is that in thee now which can believe, since thy husband is present.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast begun to be present with the understanding when thou calledst me a prophet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Woman, believe me; for if ye believe not, ye will not understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “Woman, believe me, for the hour will come when ye shall neither in this mountain nor in Jerusalem worship the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye worship ye know not what: we worship what we know; for salvation is of the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the hour will come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And now is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, what hour?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth,” not in this mountain, not in the temple, but in spirit and in truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the Father seeketh such to worship Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why does the Father seek such to worship Him, not on a mountain, not in the temple, but in spirit and in truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “God is Spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If God were body, it were right that He should be worshipped on a mountain, for a mountain is corporeal; it were right He should be worshipped in the temple, for a temple is corporeal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “God is Spirit; and they that worship Him, must worship in spirit and in truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have heard, and it is manifest; we had gone out of doors, and we are sent inward.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would I could find, thou didst say, some high and lonely mountain!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I think that, because God is on high, He hears me the rather from a high place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because thou art on a mountain, dost thou imagine thyself near to God, and that He will quickly hear thee, as if calling to Him from the nearest place?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He dwells on high, but regards the lowly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Lord is near.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the high, perhaps?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To them who are contrite of heart.” ’Tis a wonderful thing: He dwelleth on high, and yet is near to the lowly; “He hath regard to lowly things, but lofty things He knoweth from afar;” He seeth the proud afar off, and He is the less near to them the higher they appear to themselves to be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Didst thou seek a mountain, then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come down, that thou mayest come near Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But wouldest thou ascend?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ascend, but do not seek a mountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The ascents,” it saith, “are in his heart, in the valley of weeping.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The valley is humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore do all within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even if perhaps thou seekest some lofty place, some holy place, make thyself a temple for God within time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the temple of God is holy, which temple are ye.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wouldest thou pray in a temple?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pray in thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But be thou first a temple of God, for He in His temple heareth him that prays.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We worship that which we know: ye worship ye know not what; for salvation is of the Jews.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great thing has He attributed to the Jews; but do not understand Him to mean those spurious Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand that wall to which another is joined, that they may be joined together, resting on the corner-stone, which is Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there is one wall from the Jews, another from the Gentiles; these walls are far apart, only until they are united in the Corner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now the aliens were strangers and foreigners from the covenants of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According to this, it is said, “We worship what we know.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is said, indeed, in the person of the Jews, but not of all Jews, not of reprobate Jews, but of such as were the apostles, as were the prophets, as were all those saints who sold all their goods, and laid the price of their goods at the apostles’ feet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For God hath not rejected His people which He foreknew.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The woman heard this, and proceeded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She had already called Him a prophet; she observes that He with whom she was speaking uttered such things as still more pertained to the prophet; and what answer did she make?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See: “The woman saith unto Him, I know that Messias will come, who is called Christ: when He then is come, He will show us all things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just now she saith, The Jews are contending for the temple, and we for this mountain: when He has come, He will despise the mountain, and overthrow the temple; He will teach us all things, that we may know how to worship in spirit and in truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She knew who could teach her, but she did not yet know Him that was now teaching her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now she was worthy to receive the manifestation of Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now Messias is Anointed: Anointed, in Greek, is Christ; in Hebrew, Messias; whence also, in Punic, Messe means Anoint.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Hebrew, Punic and Syriac are cognate and neighboring languages.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, “The woman saith unto Him, I know that Messias will come, who is called Christ: when He then is come, He will tell us all things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus saith unto her, I that speak with thee am He.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She called her husband; he is made the head of the woman, and Christ is made the head of the man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now is the woman constituted in faith, and ruled, as about to live rightly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After she heard this, “I that speak with thee am He,” what further could she say, when the Lord Jesus willed to manifest Himself to the woman, to whom He had said, “Believe me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And immediately came His disciples, and marvelled that He talked with the woman.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He was seeking her that was lost, He who came to seek that which was lost: they marvelled at this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They marvelled at a good thing, they were not suspecting an evil thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Yet no man said, What seekest Thou, or why talkest Thou with her?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The woman then left her water-pot.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Having heard, “I that speak with thee am He,” and having received Christ the Lord into her heart, what could she do but now leave her water-pot, and run to preach the gospel?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She cast out lust, and hastened to proclaim the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them who would preach the gospel learn; let them throw away their water-pot at the well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You remember what I said before of the water-pot: it was a vessel with which the water was drawn, called hydria, from its Greek name, because water is hydor in Greek; just as if it were called aquarium, from the Latin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She threw away her water-pot then, which was no longer of use, but a burden to her, such was her avidity to be satisfied with that water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Throwing her burden away, to make known Christ, “she ran to the city, and says to those men, Come, and see a man that told me all things that ever I did.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Step by step, lest those men should get angry and indignant, and should persecute her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Is this Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then they went out of the city, and came to Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And in the meanwhile His disciples besought Him, saying, Master, eat.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they had gone to buy meat, and had returned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But He said, I have meat to eat which ye know not of.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought Him aught to eat?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What wonder if that woman did not understand about the water?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See; the disciples do not yet understand the meat.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He heard their thoughts, and now as a master instructs them, not in a round-about way, as He did the woman while He still sought her husband, but openly at once: “My meat,” saith He, “is to do the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, in the case of that woman, it was even His drink to do the will of Him that sent Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That was the reason why He said, “I thirst, give me to drink;” namely, to work faith in her, and to drink of her faith, and to transplant her into His own body, for His body is the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “My meat is to do the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Say ye not, that there are yet four months, and then cometh harvest?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was aglow for the work, and was arranging to send forth laborers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You count four months to the harvest; I show you another harvest, white and ready.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, I say unto you, “Lift up your eyes, and see that the fields are already white for the harvest.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He is going to send forth the reapers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For in this is the saying true, that one reapeth, another soweth: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have sent you to reap that on which ye have not labored: others have labored, and ye are entered into their labor.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He sent reapers; sent He not the sowers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither the reapers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where others labored already.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where labor had already been bestowed, surely there had been sowing; and what had been sown had now become ripe, and required the sickle and the threshing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither, then, were the reapers to be sent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where the prophets had already preached before; for they were the sowers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had they not been the sowers, whence had this come to the woman, “I know that Messias will come”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That woman was now ripened fruit, and the harvest fields were white, and sought the sickle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I sent you,” then.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To reap what ye have not sown: others sowed, and ye are entered into their labors.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who labored?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Read their labors; in all their labors there is a prophecy of Christ, and for that reason they were sowers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moses, and all the other patriarchs, and all the prophets, how much they suffered in that cold season when they sowed!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore was the harvest now ready in Judea.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Justly was the corn there said to be as it were ripe, when so many thousands of men brought the price of their goods, and, laying them at the apostles’ feet, having eased their shoulders of this worldly baggage, began to follow the Lord Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Verily the harvest was ripe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was made of it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that harvest a few grains were thrown out, and sowed the whole world; and another harvest is rising which is to be reaped in the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of that harvest it is said, “They that sow in tears shall reap with joy.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But to that harvest not apostles, but angels, shall be sent forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The reapers,” saith He, “are the angels.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That harvest, then, is growing among tares, and is awaiting to be purged in the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that harvest to which the disciples were sent first, where the prophets labored, was already ripe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet, brethren, observe what was said: “may rejoice together, both he that soweth and he that reapeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They had dissimilar labors in time, but the rejoicing they shall enjoy alike equally; they shall receive for their wages together eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And many Samaritans of that city believed on Him, because of the saying of the woman, who testified, He told me all that ever I did.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when the Samaritans came to Him, they besought Him that He would tarry with them; and He tarried there two days.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And many more believed because of His word; and said to the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy words; for we have heard Him ourselves, and we know that this is indeed the Saviour of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This also must be slightly noticed, for the lesson is come to an end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The woman first announced Him, and the Samaritans believed her testimony; and they besought Him to stay with them, and He stayed there two days, and many more believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when they had believed, they said to the woman, “Now we believe, not because of thy word; but we are come to know Him ourselves, and we know that this is indeed the Saviour of the world:” first by report, then by His presence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So it is to-day with them that are without, and are not yet Christians.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ is made known to them by Christian friends; and just upon the report of that woman, that is, the Church, they come to Christ, they believe through this report.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He stays with them two days, that is, gives them two precepts of charity; and many more believe, and more firmly believe, on Him, because He is in truth the Saviour of the world.
